title: If as Expression
description: Demonstrate how Kotlin's if statement can return a value directly.
problemStatement:
In Kotlin, `if` is an expression! Assign the result of an `if-else` block to a variable `max`. If `a = 10` is greater than `b = 20`, assign `a`, otherise `b`. Print `max`.

**Expected Output:** `20`.
hints: Syntax: `val result = if (condition) { a } else { b }`.
